metadata.title: The Silent Radix: Positional Notation as Ultrametric Tree and the Calculus of Indications as Remedy
metadata.description:
A consilient research program demonstrating that positional notation is an ultrametric tree whose radix encodes a chosen grouping cycle; that the systematic errors of silent radices, silent metrics, and assumed linearity arise from substituting a monocultural decimal default and flattening the tree into an Archimedean line; and that re-founding on Spencer-Brown's calculus of indications restores the observer, the ultrametric, and the self-measuring '10' as the seed of all self-aware quantity.

Deliverables:
- Consequence Atlas: 65 documented silent-frame errors across computing, science, cognition, and history
- Synthesis Paper: 9-section research paper with literature anchoring
- Formal Appendix: 8 theorems including Silent Radix Fixed-Point Lemma and Observer Necessity Theorem
- Explicit Frame Pattern Language: 9 design patterns for self-aware numeric communication
- LoF Number Builder Specification: Interactive web application design
- Cross-Reference Index: Bidirectional Atlas-to-paper claim mapping
metadata.notes: One-month research sprint. Single researcher with LLM orchestration. All claims anchored in published literature. Consequence Atlas contains 65 verified silent-frame errors. Formal Appendix contains 8 theorems with proof sketches. Companion interactive tool specification (LoF Number Builder) included.
